title: Allocation of heat and energy efficiency related costs in rented non-domestic buildings
body: "This research builds upon the existing evidence base on how costs and responsibilities are split between landlords and tenants regarding space heating (including hot water generation) and cooling and energy efficiency improvements. It covers :   operational costs, such as energy bills, maintaining and repairing heating systems, choosing an energy supplier / tariff, and metering responsibilities capital costs, such as installing new or replacing existing infrastructure in the building   Research took place between December 2021 and May 2022:   survey covering 600 tenants interviews with 37 landlords, tenants, and non-domestic leasing experts / legal representatives   It covered:   all sectors different sizes of organisations on/off-gas grid premises different lease lengths, building ages, Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) ratings, lease years single/ mixed-use buildings   "
